scenario: Per-route stores vs single global store.
context: App with 30 routes, each with route-specific state.
sam_initial_position: Single store. Easier to reason about.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Route-specific state lives with route. Mount/unmount handles lifecycle.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Some state spans routes (filters that persist).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: That's app state, not route state. Different store.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. App store + route stores.

context_dependent_answer: App store for cross-route. Local state (useState/Zustand) for route.
decision_framework: debate-201: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Per-route stores vs single global store.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors put route state in global store. Senior insight: state has lifecycle. Match lifecycle to state.

scenario: Slot pattern via React.Children vs context.
context: Layout component with named slots (header, sidebar, main).
alex_initial_position: Context. Children declare slot name, layout finds them.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Slot via context allows JSX-natural composition.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Named props are explicit. Reader sees structure.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Slot allows conditional rendering of slots.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Named props with undefined check do that.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For deep nesting, slot pattern reads better.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For shallow, named props clearer.

context_dependent_answer: Shallow: named props. Deep: slot pattern.
decision_framework: debate-202: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Slot pattern via React.Children vs context.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors children-only. Senior insight: named slots clarify intent.

scenario: Form submission UX: full-page reload vs SPA-style.
alex_initial_position: SPA-style. Preserve scroll, show in-page feedback.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: SPA preserves context. User sees inline result.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Redirect to success page is clearer separation.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For inline edits (settings page), redirect is jarring.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For new resource creation, redirect to created.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Pattern: redirect on create, inline on edit.

context_dependent_answer: Edit: inline. Create: redirect.
decision_framework: debate-203: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: Form submission UX: full-page reload vs SPA-style.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors pick one pattern for all. Senior insight: edit and create have different UX needs.

scenario: WebSocket reconnection: native vs library (reconnecting-websocket, socket.io).
alex_initial_position: Library. Reconnection is non-trivial - backoff, heartbeats, message queueing.
sam_initial_position: Native WebSocket + custom retry. Less dep.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Reconnect logic edge cases: exponential backoff, jitter, max retries, network detection.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Library buys 200KB. Custom is 50 LOC.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: reconnecting-websocket is 5KB. Cheap.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Library or write 100 LOC carefully.

context_dependent_answer: Production WebSocket: library or careful custom. Toy/demo: native fine.
decision_framework: debate-204: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: WebSocket reconnection: native vs library (reconnecting-websocket, socket.io).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't handle reconnect. Senior insight: networks fail. WebSocket reconnect is mandatory.

scenario: oklch() vs rgb() / hex for color tokens.
context: Design tokens for modern color management.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: oklch lightness is perceptual. 0.5 = mid-gray regardless of hue. With hex, 'mid lightness' varies.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Designers think in hex. Conversion friction.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Figma supports oklch now.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Browser support? Old browsers fall back.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: All major browsers since 2023.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Adopt oklch for design tokens.

context_dependent_answer: Modern projects: oklch. Legacy: hex.
decision_framework: debate-205: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: oklch() vs rgb() / hex for color tokens.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors stick with hex. Senior insight: oklch enables perceptual scales that 'just look right'.

scenario: prefers-reduced-motion: replace with fade or skip animation.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Instant transitions feel abrupt. Fade is subtle, non-vestibular.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: User asked for reduced motion. Respect literally.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: WCAG: reduce motion that triggers vestibular. Opacity doesn't.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Opacity-only acceptable.

context_dependent_answer: Reduced motion: opacity-only or instant. No motion (translate/scale).
decision_framework: debate-206: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: prefers-reduced-motion: replace with fade or skip animation.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors ignore reduced motion. Senior insight: vestibular disorders are real. Translate/scale animations trigger them.

scenario: Visual regression: full page snapshot vs component snapshot.
sam_initial_position: Full page snapshots. Catch integration issues.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Component snapshots catch issue without false positives from unrelated changes.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Full page catches layout interactions component snapshots miss.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Both. Component for design system, full page for app.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Design system: component. App: full page on key screens.
decision_framework: debate-207: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: Visual regression: full page snapshot vs component snapshot.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't visual regression. Senior insight: CSS bugs are silent. Visual regression is the only catch.

scenario: Type narrowing: in/typeof/instanceof vs custom predicates.
alex_initial_position: Built-in narrowing (in, typeof) first. Custom predicates for complex.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: in operator narrows for discriminated unions naturally.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Custom predicate function reads as intent.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For one-off: in. For reuse: predicate.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

where_they_agree: Discriminated union narrows on type field
context_dependent_answer: in/typeof: one-off. Predicates: reused checks.
decision_framework: debate-208: judge the typescript strictness debate in this scenario: Type narrowing: in/typeof/instanceof vs custom predicates.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use 'as' casts. Senior insight: narrowing is type-safe; casting isn't.

scenario: Code-splitting third-party deps separately.
alex_initial_position: Separate vendor chunk per major dep group. Cache stability.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: React updates rarely. UI lib updates monthly. Separate chunks → React stays cached.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Multiple chunks = multiple requests. HTTP/2 helps but still.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: HTTP/2 multiplexes. Multiple requests trivial.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Group deps by update frequency.

context_dependent_answer: Production: group by update frequency.
decision_framework: debate-209: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Code-splitting third-party deps separately.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors single vendor chunk. Senior insight: cache stability is a win. Group by update rate.

scenario: ESM vs CommonJS for libraries.
alex_initial_position: Dual: ESM + CJS. Compat with both.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Node CJS users still exist. Dual covers all.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: ESM is the future. Dual is maintenance burden.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: package.json 'exports' field handles dual cleanly.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Dual via exports.

context_dependent_answer: Library: dual ESM + CJS for compat. Modern app: ESM only.
decision_framework: debate-210: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: ESM vs CommonJS for libraries.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors single format. Senior insight: libraries serve consumers in both module systems.

scenario: Internal component lib: separate package vs in monorepo.
sam_initial_position: Monorepo workspace. No versioning, live updates.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Versioning lets teams adopt at their pace.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Monorepo means all updates atomic. No drift.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Drift is OK for non-breaking. Breaking changes need version.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For internal lib, mass-update on breaking is fine.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For small monorepo: workspace. For large: separate package.

context_dependent_answer: 5-team: workspace. 50-team: versioned package.
decision_framework: debate-211: judge the component library strategy debate in this scenario: Internal component lib: separate package vs in monorepo.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors npm publish internal libs. Senior insight: monorepo workspace eliminates publish cycle for internal.

scenario: User-visible errors: technical message vs friendly.
sam_initial_position: Show error code for support. 'Error: NETWORK_TIMEOUT. Contact support.'

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Technical errors confuse most users.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For support, error code helps diagnose.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Code in details, friendly headline.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. Headline friendly, expandable details.

context_dependent_answer: Headline: friendly. Details: technical + code.
decision_framework: debate-212: judge the error boundary strategy debate in this scenario: User-visible errors: technical message vs friendly.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors show raw errors. Senior insight: error UX is design. Users need reassurance, support needs diagnostics.

scenario: Lazy-load translations (i18n) per route.
context: App with 10 locales, lots of strings.
sam_initial_position: All translations upfront. Avoid loading delay.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Translations for unrelated routes shouldn't ship.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: On route change, fetching translations delays UI.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Prefetch route translations during idle.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Or namespace-based: common + per-route. Common upfront.

context_dependent_answer: Common upfront + route-namespaced + prefetch.
decision_framework: debate-213: judge the code splitting philosophy debate in this scenario: Lazy-load translations (i18n) per route.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors load all locales. Senior insight: translations are routes' data too. Split them.

scenario: Touch targets: 44px (Apple) vs 48dp (Material) vs 24px (compact UI).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: WCAG 2.5.5: 44x44 minimum. Mobile critical.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For desktop tools (CAD, IDE), density wins.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Desktop: 24px OK. Mobile/touch: 44+.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. Device-aware sizing.

context_dependent_answer: Touch: 44dp. Desktop power-user: 24px+ with hover targets.
decision_framework: debate-214: judge the accessibility tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Touch targets: 44px (Apple) vs 48dp (Material) vs 24px (compact UI).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use one size. Senior insight: touch and mouse have different ergonomics.

scenario: Adapter pattern for external APIs.
context: Multiple payment providers (Stripe, Paddle, Lemon Squeezy).
alex_initial_position: Adapter interface. Components don't know which provider.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Adapter enables swap or A/B testing providers.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Adapters often over-abstract; concrete code clearer.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For payment specifically, swap is realistic (price negotiations).

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted for payment. Otherwise concrete.

context_dependent_answer: Swap-likely (payment): adapter. Fixed: concrete.
decision_framework: debate-215: judge the frontend architecture patterns debate in this scenario: Adapter pattern for external APIs.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors couple to vendor SDK. Senior insight: adapters are insurance against vendor changes.

scenario: Derived state: useMemo vs computed (Jotai/Zustand selector).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Selector cached across components.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: useMemo simpler. Component-local.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For shared derived state, selector. Else useMemo.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Shared: selector. Local: useMemo.
decision_framework: debate-216: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Derived state: useMemo vs computed (Jotai/Zustand selector).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors useMemo for derived. Senior insight: shared derived state benefits from library selectors.

scenario: Provider proliferation: many providers vs combined.
context: App has Theme, Auth, I18n, Toast, Modal providers.
alex_initial_position: Combine into one AppProvider. Cleaner App.tsx.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: App.tsx with 5 nested providers is ugly.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Combined provider couples concerns.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Compose-helpers exist (provider composition).

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Use compose helper for cleanliness without coupling.

context_dependent_answer: Many providers: compose helper. Few: nest.
decision_framework: debate-217: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Provider proliferation: many providers vs combined.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors nest visibly. Senior insight: compose helper is cleaner when many providers.

scenario: Form field auto-focus on mount.
sam_initial_position: Conditional. Auto-focus modals/dialogs, not page loads.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: User opened the modal to type. Focus is intent.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For page-load forms, screen reader users get interrupted.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. Modal: auto-focus. Page: only if form is the page.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Modal: auto-focus. Page form (if dominant): auto-focus. Embedded: don't.
decision_framework: debate-218: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: Form field auto-focus on mount.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors auto-focus everywhere. Senior insight: focus is also interruption.

scenario: Pagination: offset/limit vs cursor.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Offset shifts when items inserted. User sees duplicates.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For static lists, offset fine.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Frequently inserted: cursor required.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Active feed: cursor. Admin tables: offset.
decision_framework: debate-219: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: Pagination: offset/limit vs cursor.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use offset always. Senior insight: offset is for static; active data needs cursor.

scenario: Logical properties (margin-inline) vs physical (margin-left).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Logical properties auto-handle RTL. Less code.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Less familiar to many devs.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Tailwind has logical utilities (ms-/me-/ps-/pe-).

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Logical for RTL apps.

context_dependent_answer: RTL support: logical (ms-4 etc.). LTR-only: physical fine.
decision_framework: debate-220: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: Logical properties (margin-inline) vs physical (margin-left).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use ml-/mr- everywhere. Senior insight: logical properties future-proof for RTL.

scenario: Animation perf budget: 60fps vs 120fps.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 60fps is universal. 120fps optimization premature.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: 120fps displays now common. Sticking to 60fps wastes hardware.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: requestAnimationFrame adapts. Code at 60fps runs fine at 120Hz.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. 60fps target, hardware adapts up.

context_dependent_answer: 60fps target. Browsers adapt to display refresh.

weaknesses: Slightly more work at high refresh

decision_framework: debate-221: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: Animation perf budget: 60fps vs 120fps.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors hardcode 60. Senior insight: rAF adapts. Code is refresh-rate agnostic.

scenario: Component testing isolation: shallow render vs full mount with mocked context.
alex_initial_position: Full mount + test wrapper that provides context.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Full mount tests real behavior, including context interaction.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Shallow faster, focused.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Full mount with test wrapper is the modern pattern.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Full mount + test-utils.

context_dependent_answer: Full mount + custom render with providers.
decision_framework: debate-222: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: Component testing isolation: shallow render vs full mount with mocked context.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors mock everything. Senior insight: test wrapper provides realistic context with mocks where needed.

scenario: Strict null checks across boundaries.
context: Functions accepting nullable parameters from many call sites.
alex_initial_position: Tighten signatures. Require non-null at entry.
sam_initial_position: Accept null, handle internally. Caller convenience.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Tight signatures push null handling to caller. Each caller has context.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Many callers means many null checks. DRY violation.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For functions with semantic 'requires X', tight.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For utility, accept null and short-circuit.

context_dependent_answer: Required: tight. Utility: accept null.
decision_framework: debate-223: judge the typescript strictness debate in this scenario: Strict null checks across boundaries.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors accept null everywhere. Senior insight: signatures are contracts. Don't accept what makes no sense.

scenario: Sharded state across multiple stores vs single store.
context: 30+ components subscribe to different parts of state.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Sharded means subscribers only see their data. No re-render on unrelated changes.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: With selectors, single store achieves same.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Selector overhead grows. Sharded is O(1) subscribe.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For 30 components, overhead negligible.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For 300, sharded matters.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. 30: single. 300: sharded.

context_dependent_answer: 30 components: single store + selectors. 300+: sharded.
decision_framework: debate-224: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Sharded state across multiple stores vs single store.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors put everything in one store. Senior insight: scale dictates sharding.

scenario: Bun bundler vs Vite for new project.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Vite plugins handle nearly everything. Bun ecosystem smaller.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Bun's all-in-one (runtime + bundler + test) reduces tools.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Vite + Vitest + esbuild is the de facto standard.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Vite default. Bun experimental.

context_dependent_answer: Production: Vite. Greenfield/experimental: Bun.
decision_framework: debate-225: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: Bun bundler vs Vite for new project.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors chase the fastest. Senior insight: ecosystem maturity beats raw speed for production.
